lines.raw_text: This approach leverages low absorption in the blue-green spectrum (around 450-550 nm) for higher bandwidth and low latency, though range is typically under 100-200 m due to scattering and absorption in water.[3][5] Recent advancements include MIT Lincoln Laboratory's eye-safe laser system for long-distance undersea links supporting high-definition video at rates over 5,000 times faster than acoustics, with low power and stealth features.[1] Kyocera demonstrated 5.2 Gbps short-range transmission for real-time video and sensor data in ocean exploration.[2]

lines.raw_text: **Underwater robots primarily communicate in real-time using acoustic methods, as sound waves propagate effectively over long distances underwater, unlike radio waves which attenuate rapidly due to seawater's conductivity.** [2][4]

lines.tagged_text: <null>**Key aspects include:**</null>

lines.raw_text: - **Acoustic modems and signal processing:** Robots use acoustic modems to encode data into sound signals for transmission and decode received signals to extract commands, sensor data, or updates, enabling communication up to several kilometers despite challenges like temperature, salinity, noise, and multipath effects. [2][4]

[2][4]</null>

lines.raw_text: **Reducing the cost of offshore oil and gas structural inspections can be achieved through several strategies, with strong evidence supporting the use of advanced technologies like drones, AI-driven analytics, and predictive monitoring.**

spans.text: **Reducing the cost of offshore oil and gas structural inspections can be achieved through several strategies, with strong evidence supporting the use of advanced technologies like drones, AI-driven analytics, and predictive monitoring.**

lines.raw_text: ### 1. **Use of Advanced Technologies**
lines.tagged_text: <null>### 1. **Use of Advanced Technologies**</null>

lines.raw_text: Drones and remotely operated vehicles (ROVs) enable inspections of hard-to-reach offshore areas, reducing personnel travel, safety risks, and costs by up to 50% while increasing efficiency by 33%[1][2][3]. Next-generation drones provide real-time HD video, ultrasonic testing, and 3D laser scanning with 2mm accuracy, minimizing onshore team sizes and enabling faster reporting[3].

lines.raw_text: ## Key Advantages of PMSMs for Subsea Use
lines.tagged_text: <null>## Key Advantages of PMSMs for Subsea Use</null>

spans.text: ## Key Advantages of PMSMs for Subsea Use

lines.raw_text: **Efficiency and Power Density**
lines.tagged_text: <null>**Efficiency and Power Density**</null>

lines.raw_text: PMSMs can achieve maximum efficiencies exceeding 95% at full load for larger power motors[4], with high power density that allows them to deliver more power per unit weight[5]. This efficiency stems from their design: the rotor's magnetic field is generated by permanent magnets built during manufacturing, eliminating the need for external field induction and resulting in no slip losses like induction motors[5].

lines.raw_text: **Reliability in Subsea Environments**
lines.tagged_text: <null>**Reliability in Subsea Environments**</null>

lines.raw_text: For subsea multiphase boosting applications, PMSMs provide significant advantages over traditional machinery[1]. The larger liquid-filled gap between rotor and stator results in significantly lower drag losses, enabling operation at higher speeds than liquid-filled asynchronous machines[1]. This design reduces failure points and maintenance needs compared to systems requiring gearboxes or mechanical transmissions.

spans.text: Thrusters designed for low-drag and high-efficiency subsea movement typically feature advanced design elements such as **rim-driven** and **hubless propellers**, electric propulsion systems, hydrodynamic optimization, seawater-lubricated bearings, and low acoustic signatures. lines.raw_text: - **Rim-driven and Hubless Designs**: These thrusters have ultra-slim, brushless motors with hubless propellers that prevent entanglement and reduce drag.[1][4][6] Companies such as **[Hydromea](https://www.hydromea.com)** (DISKDRIVE™ series),[4] **[Copenhagen Subsea](https://copenhagensubsea.com)** (rim-driven electric thrusters),[2] **[Rim Drive Technology](https://rimdrivetechnology.nl)** (subsea bow thrusters),[3] **[TSL Technology](http://www.tsltechnology.com)** (IntegratedThrusters™),[6] **[Kongsberg](https://www.kongsberg.com)** (AZ-PM and TT-PM thrusters),[5] and **[Alva Industries](https://www.alvaindustries.com)** (Hydra-100) use this design to enhance efficiency and reliability.[7]

lines.raw_text: **Yes, wireless underwater video transmission is possible primarily through acoustic and optical methods, with acoustic being the most common due to sound's propagation in water, though it faces bandwidth and latency limits.** [1][2]
lines.tagged_text: <null>**Yes, wireless underwater video transmission is possible primarily through acoustic and optical methods, with acoustic being the most common due to sound's propagation in water, though it faces bandwidth and latency limits.** [1][2]</null>

lines.raw_text: **Key methods include:**
lines.tagged_text: <null>**Key methods include:**</null>

lines.raw_text: - **Acoustic communication**: Uses sound waves with modems achieving up to 150 kbps via high-efficiency modulation like 64-QAM at 75 kHz carrier frequency, enabling compressed real-time video over short vertical paths; prototype systems reached 30 kbps in deep water. Advanced techniques like 3D CNN autoencoders, LSTM-based encoding with OFDM, and analog schemes double frame rates under bandwidth constraints for submersibles and IoT. [1][2]

lines.raw_text: **Acoustic communication** is indeed the most established technology for underwater networks[2]. It achieves communication up to several kilometers because sound propagation loss in seawater is significantly smaller than electromagnetic waves[2]. However, the tradeoff is real: acoustic systems suffer from low bandwidth, high latency, and environmental noise sensitivity[1].

lines.raw_text: **Encapsulated motors improve the maintenance cycle of underwater propulsion units primarily by enhancing protection and reliability.** Here’s how they contribute[1][4][5]:
lines.tagged_text: <null>**Encapsulated motors improve the maintenance cycle of underwater propulsion units primarily by enhancing protection and reliability.** Here’s how they contribute[1][4][5]:</null>

spans.text: **Encapsulated motors improve the maintenance cycle of underwater propulsion units primarily by enhancing protection and reliability.** Here’s how they contribute[1][4][5]:

lines.raw_text: 1. **Protection from Water Ingress:** Encapsulation seals the motor components, preventing water, moisture, and contaminants from entering. This reduces the risk of corrosion and electrical short circuits, common issues in underwater environments[1].
lines.tagged_text: <null>1. **Protection from Water Ingress:** Encapsulation seals the motor components, preventing water, moisture, and contaminants from entering. This reduces the risk of corrosion and electrical short circuits, common issues in underwater environments[1].</null>

spans.text: 1. **Protection from Water Ingress:** Encapsulation seals the motor components, preventing water, moisture, and contaminants from entering. This reduces the risk of corrosion and electrical short circuits, common issues in underwater environments[1].

lines.raw_text: 2. **Reduced Wear and Tear:** By isolating the motor windings and other sensitive parts from harsh external conditions, encapsulated motors experience less mechanical and chemical degradation, extending their operational life[1][5].
lines.tagged_text: <null>2. **Reduced Wear and Tear:** By isolating the motor windings and other sensitive parts from harsh external conditions, encapsulated motors experience less mechanical and chemical degradation, extending their operational life[1][5].</null>

spans.text: 2. **Reduced Wear and Tear:** By isolating the motor windings and other sensitive parts from harsh external conditions, encapsulated motors experience less mechanical and chemical degradation, extending their operational life[1][5].

lines.raw_text: 3. **Lower Maintenance Frequency:** With better protection, these motors require less frequent inspections, cleaning, and repairs, thus lengthening the intervals between maintenance cycles[1][4].

lines.raw_text: 1. **Fiber Optic Cables:** The most reliable and high-bandwidth method is to use subsea fiber optic cables connecting the sensor station to an onshore facility or a surface buoy. Fiber optics support very high data rates and long-distance transmission with minimal loss[1].

lines.raw_text: 2. **Acoustic Modems:** For wireless communication underwater where cables are impractical, acoustic modems are used. However, their data rates are much lower compared to fiber optics, typically in the kbps range, making them less suitable for very high-speed offloading[1].

lines.raw_text: **Acoustic Modem Communication** is the most established approach. Sonardyne's subsea Acoustic Data Logger systems use "spread spectrum, high speed acoustic link to allow large volumes of data to be rapidly and accurately retrieved topside" through a vessel, rig, or Uncrewed Surface Vehicle (USV)[4]. Water Linked acoustic modems similarly enable "reliable underwater communication for ROVs, AUVs, and divers, transmitting data over long distances in tough conditions"[3].

Water Linked acoustic modems similarly enable "reliable underwater communication for ROVs, AUVs, and divers, transmitting data over long distances in tough conditions"[3].

lines.raw_text: **ROV-Based Data Transfer** is widely deployed in practice. ROVs equipped with optical data telemetry modems (such as LUMA modems) can provide significantly more reliable connections than acoustic alternatives in noisy installation environments. These modems "transmit data at a much higher bandwidth than acoustic telemetry systems allowing multiple data set updates per second compared to one update every 3-5 seconds"[1], with operating ranges of 7-50 meters depending on modem version[1].

lines.raw_text: **Hybrid and Optical Systems** offer alternatives for specific conditions. Optical communications can "provide high data rates" but are "limited by water clarity and only effective over short distances"[5]. Hybrid systems combining acoustic and optical methods leverage the strengths of each—using acoustics for long-range communication and optical signals for high-bandwidth, short-range data transfer[5].
